---
title: "Reproducibility_appendix"
author: "Madeline Frazer"
bibliography: Reproducibility.bib
csl: chicago-fullnote-bibliography.csl 
output: pdf_document
---

This page was last updated on `r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')`.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Background

This document outlines the steps of all analyses included in my project, "Plant abundance as a function of predator exclusion in the Chihuahua desert," including all data processing, model fitting, and figure generation.

The data is longitudinal ecological data (1977-2002) by [@ernest2009]. It is publicly accessible, accessed [here](https://www.esapubs.org/archive/ecol/E090/118/metadata.htm).

These data are comprised of plant species abundance data, precipitation data, and binary treatment data (predator presence/absence) created from the table found in Table 1 of the online [metadata](https://esapubs.org/archive/ecol/E090/118/metadata.htm) (see Data Import). These data were collected on 24 plots in the Chihuahua desert near Portal, Arizona, USA, with 1 precipitation data point for all plots.

# Data Import

Download CSV files from above site (plant and precipitation data).

For treatments, a qualitative table of the treatments each plot received on which years can be found in the metadata for this [data set](https://www.esapubs.org/archive/ecol/E090/118/metadata.htm). This project includes a copy of that qualitative table in CSV form (plot_treatments). It also includes a quantitative version, where each unique combination of plot and year has corresponding binary values representing the inclusion or exclusion of kangaroo rats (large rodents), all rodents, *Pogonomyrmex rugosus* (a granivorous ant species), or all ants. 0 = excluded (not present), 1 = included (present). This table is what is used in the following analyses.

# Data Cleaning

While the longitudinal study went from 1977-2002, we only used data from 1989–2002 because that is when plant species-level abundances were consistently recorded.

## Plants

In this step, we create a sum of total species detected in each plot per year (combing winter/summer and annual/perennial) and filter to years 1989-2002.

1.  Load packages 'dplyr', 'tidyr', and 'here'.

```{R}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(here)
```

2.  Read in the four plant CSV files

```{R}
#Download the CSV files and insert your path to the files here
plants_1 <- read.csv(here("path", "to", "data", "Portal_plant_summer_annual_19892002.csv"))
plants_2 <- read.csv(here("path", "to", "data", "Portal_plant_summer_perennial_19892002.csv"))
plants_3 <- read.csv(here("path", "to", "data", "Portal_plant_winter_annual_19892002.csv"))
plants_4 <- read.csv(here("path", "to", "data", "Portal_plant_winter_perennial_19892002.csv"))
```

3.  Calculate total abundance per plot per year

```{R}
#Pivot tables to be long ("pivot_longer") while excluding columns "Season", "Life.History", "Year", "Plot", and "Quadrat."
long_plants_1 <- plants_1 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = !c("Season", "Life.History", "Year", "Plot", "Quadrat"),
    names_to = "species",
    values_to = "abundance")    # Change column names to "species" and "abundance."
long_plants_1 <- long_plants_1 %>% mutate(abundance = na_if(abundance, -99))      #Change all "-99" values in "abundance" column to NAs (was not measured). 

long_plants_2 <- plants_2 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = !c("Season", "Life.History", "Year", "Plot", "Quadrat"),
    names_to = "species",
    values_to = "abundance")
long_plants_2 <- long_plants_2 %>% mutate(abundance = na_if(abundance, -99))

long_plants_3 <- plants_3 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = !c("Season", "Life.History", "Year", "Plot", "Quadrat"),
    names_to = "species",
    values_to = "abundance")
long_plants_3 <- long_plants_3 %>% mutate(abundance = na_if(abundance, -99))

long_plants_4 <- plants_4 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = !c("Season", "Life.History", "Year", "Plot", "Quadrat"),
    names_to = "species",
    values_to = "abundance")
long_plants_4 <- long_plants_4 %>% mutate(abundance = na_if(abundance, -99))
```

4.  Create a column for total species abundance per plot per year

```{R}
total_abundance_1 <- long_plants_1 %>% 
  group_by(Plot, Year) %>% #group by Plot and Year
  summarize(
    total_abundance = sum(abundance, na.rm = TRUE), #exclude NAs
    n_species = n(),
    .groups = "drop"    #drop original abundance column
  )

total_abundance_2 <- long_plants_2 %>% 
  group_by(Plot, Year) %>% 
  summarize(
    total_abundance = sum(abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_species = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

total_abundance_3 <- long_plants_3 %>% 
  group_by(Plot, Year) %>% 
  summarize(
    total_abundance = sum(abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_species = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

total_abundance_4 <- long_plants_4 %>% 
  group_by(Plot, Year) %>% 
  summarize(
    total_abundance = sum(abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_species = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

5.  Combine 4 data frames into 1 data frame, summing all species abundances

```{R}
plants <- merge(total_abundance_1, total_abundance_2, by = c("Plot","Year"), all = TRUE)   #merge the first two data frames using Plot and Year, keeping all columns
plants <- plants %>%  mutate(total_abundance = total_abundance.x + total_abundance.y, .keep = "unused")   #create a new column that is the sum of the two abundance columns of the previous 2 data frames, and only keeping the new column
plants <- plants %>%  mutate(total_species = n_species.x + n_species.y, .keep = "unused")    #create a new column that is the sum of the two species count columns and keeping only the new column

plants <- merge(plants, total_abundance_3, by = c("Plot", "Year"), all = TRUE)
plants <- plants %>%  mutate(abundance = total_abundance.x + total_abundance.y, .keep = "unused")
plants <- plants %>%  mutate(total_species = n_species + total_species, .keep = "unused")

plants <- merge(plants, total_abundance_4, by = c("Plot", "Year"), all = TRUE)
plants <- plants %>%  mutate(total_abundance = total_abundance + abundance, .keep = "unused")
plants <- plants %>%  mutate(total_species = n_species + total_species, .keep = "unused")
```

6.  Create an "average abundance per species" column.

```{R}
plants <- plants %>% mutate(avg_abundance = total_abundance/total_species)    #sum of total abundance divided by the sum of species counts
```

7.  Filter to years 1989-2002

```{R}
plants_clean <- filter(plants, Year >= 1989)
```

## Precipitation

1.  Load packages (dplyr, here)
```{R}
#load packages
library(dplyr)
library(here)
```

2.  Read in the two precipitation files (different time periods with different measurement frequencies).
```{R}
#replace with path to your file
precip_1 <- read.csv(here("path", "to", "file", "Portal_precipitation_19801989.csv"))
precip_2 <- read.csv(here("path", "to", "file", "Portal_precipitation_19892002.csv"))
```

3.  Change column names to be identical between both data frames. Exclude Day and Hour columns from precip2 (unique to precip2).
```{R}
precip_2 <- precip_2 %>% select(-c(Hour, Day)) #don't include Hour or Day
precip_1 <- rename(precip_1, Precipitation = precipitation) #lowercase column name
```

4.  Average precipitation over each year for each precip data frame into a new column.
```{R}
precip_1 <- precip_1 %>% group_by(Year) %>%    #group by Year
            mutate(Annual_precip = sum(Precipitation, na.remove = TRUE)) %>%    #sum precipitation for each year
            ungroup()

precip_2 <- precip_2 %>% group_by(Year) %>% 
  mutate(Annual_precip = sum(Precipitation, na.remove = TRUE)) %>% 
  ungroup()
```

5. Select Year and Annual_precip columns
```{R}
precip_1 <- precip_1 %>% select(Year, Annual_precip)
precip_2 <- precip_2 %>% select(Year, Annual_precip) 
```

6. Combine both data sets into one data frame
```{R}
precip_clean <- full_join(precip_1, precip_2)
precip_clean <- precip_clean %>% distinct()
```

7. Filter to years 1989-2002
```{R}
precip_clean <- filter(precip_clean, Year >= 1989)
```

8. Combine the 1989 columns (because 1989 was split between both data frames)
```{R}
precip_clean <- precip_clean %>% group_by(Year) %>% 
  summarize(Annual_precip = sum(Annual_precip), .groups = "drop")
```

# Analysis

## Combine data frames

1. Read in Treatment CSV file from GitHub, edit column names to match plants and precip data frame column names
```{R}
#read in file
treatments <- read.csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MkFrazer/chihuahua_plants_ants_rodents/refs/heads/main/data/data_processed/treatments.csv") 

#fix treatments column names to match
treatments <- rename(treatments, Plot = PLOT, Year = YEAR, Large_rodents = LARGE_RODENTS, All_rodents = ALL_RODENTS, PORU_ants = PORU_ANTS, All_ants = ALL_ANTS)
```

2. Combine data frames
```{R}
#combine data frames
data <- full_join(precip, plants, by = "Year")
data <- full_join(data, treatments, by = c("Plot", "Year"))

#order columns, filter to >=1989
data <- data[, c("Plot", "Year", "total_species", "total_abundance", "avg_abundance", "Large_rodents", "All_rodents", "PORU_ants", "All_ants", "Annual_precip")]
data<- filter(data, Year >= 1989)   #filter to 1989 and later
data<- data[order(data$Plot), ]  #order rows according to plot number

#make presence/absence treatments binary categories
data$Large_rodents<- as.factor(data$Large_rodents)
data$All_rodents<- as.factor(data$All_rodents)
data$PORU_ants<- as.factor(data$PORU_ants)
data$All_ants<- as.factor(data$All_ants)
```

## Visualization

Create plots comparing dependent variable (total species abundance) according to each independent variable (precipitation, large rodents, all rodents, PORU, or all ants presence/absence)
```{R}
#plot total species abundance as a factor of precip
plot(data$Annual_precip, data$total_abundance)

#plot total species abundance as a factor of large rodent presence/absence
plot(data$Large_rodents, data$total_abundance)

#plot total species abundance as a factor of all rodent presence/absence
plot(data$All_rodents, data$total_abundance)

#plot total species abundance as a factor of PORU presence/absence
plot(data$PORU_ants, data$total_abundance)

#plot total species abundance as a factor of all ants presence/absence
plot(data$All_ants, data$total_abundance)

```

## Model Comparison

1. Load packages 'nlme,' 'MuMIn,' 'MASS.'
```{R}
library(nlme)
library(MuMIn)
library(MASS)
```

2. Total species abundance is count data: compare Poisson and Negative Binomial global models using AIC to determine best model for this data.
```{R}
#create Poisson global model
global_model_P<- glm(total_abundance ~ Large_rodents + All_rodents + PORU_ants + All_ants + Annual_precip + (1|Year/Plot), 
         data = data,
         family = poisson(link="log"),   #log link
         na.action = "na.fail")

#create negative binomical global model
global_model_NB <- glm.nb(total_abundance ~ Large_rodents + All_rodents + PORU_ants + All_ants + Annual_precip + (1|Year/Plot), 
                       data = data,
                       link = log,      #log link
                       na.action = "na.fail")

#compare AIC values between Poisson and Negative Binomial models
AIC(global_model_P, global_model_NB)

#Negative binomial model was better than the Poisson
```

3. Compare all possible models from global model using dredge() to determine which factors are most important to include in base model (model0)
```{R}
all_models_NB <- dredge(global_model_NB)
print(all_models_NB)

###either all_ants or PORU_ants need to be included in final model, and Annual precip

model0 <- glm.nb(total_abundance ~ All_ants + Annual_precip + (1|Year/Plot), 
                 data = data,
                 link = log,
                 na.action = "na.fail")
```

4. Check for temporal autocorrelation
```{R}
plot(data$Year, model0$residuals)
all_resid <- residuals(model0, type = "pearson")
acf(all_resid)   #there seems to be some sort of temporal autocorrelation

#check for temporal autocorrelation within each plot
library(DHARMa)

data$all_resid <- all_resid   #put residuals into original data frame

unique_plots <- unique(data$Plot)   #new df with each plot number

par(mfrow = c(4, 6)) #24 plots
for (p in unique_plots) {
  plot_data <- data[data$Plot == p, ]
  plot_data <- data[order(plot_data$Year),]
  
  acf(plot_data$all_resid, main = paste("Plot ", p)) #include plot number in title
}
#No significant autocorrelation by plot, autocorrelation must be from something else
```
